title: Ab Initio Spinor Kadanoff-Baym Approach to Nonequilibrium Electron, Phonon and Magnon Dynamics in Itinerant Ferromagnets
abstract: This work introduces a theoretical framework based on the Kadanoff-Baym equations in spinor space to study ultrafast magnetization dynamics in itinerant ferromagnetic systems from first principles. By incorporating spin-orbit coupling into the ab initio Hamiltonian and generalizing the self-energies to include terms beyond the charge sector, I derive scattering integrals within the Markov approximation and quasiparticle renormalizations for electrons and phonons in the presence of spin-dependent effective interactions within a many-body perturbation theory approach. I explicitly discuss how magnons emerge in this framework, and derive, through suitable approximations, a close and tractable set of equations for coupled electron,phonon and magnon dynamics that can be solved from first principles. This approach allows to treat coherent and incoherent magnetic dynamics on an equal footing, paving the way for a first principles understanding of ultrafast magnetic dynamics and demagnetization, and opening to a truly predictive theory of femtomagnetism in periodic systems.
summary: This work introduces an advanced theoretical framework using Kadanoff-Baym equations to model how magnetization changes ultrafast in itinerant ferromagnets. By treating electrons, phonons, and magnons simultaneously, it provides a first-principles method to understand demagnetization. This is crucial for developing predictive theories of femtomagnetism.
main_problem: The paper aims to develop a first-principles theoretical framework to study ultrafast magnetization dynamics in itinerant ferromagnetic systems.
main_result: It derives a tractable set of coupled equations for electron, phonon, and magnon dynamics, allowing for a unified description of coherent and incoherent magnetic dynamics.
method: It employs the Kadanoff-Baym equations in spinor space, utilizing many-body perturbation theory and the Markov approximation.
model_or_system: The system is itinerant ferromagnets, modeled using an ab initio relativistic Hamiltonian that includes spin-orbit coupling (SOC). The dynamics are treated via spinor Green's functions.
key_observables: Ultrafast magnetization dynamics, demagnetization, electron density matrix, and phonon density matrix.
important_parameters: Spin-orbit coupling strength, time scales (ultrafast/femtosecond), and coupling strengths between electron, phonon, and magnon sectors.
main_assumptions: The use of the Markov approximation and various approximations (like time-locality for the screened interaction W) are necessary to close the coupled equations of motion.
figures_summary: Not specified in the notes, but the text references diagrammatic representations of the transverse particle-hole T matrix and the resulting effective electron-boson coupling.
paper_structure: The paper builds from the general Kadanoff-Baym formalism in spinor space, incorporates SOC effects, derives equations for electron and phonon dynamics, and finally formulates the coupled equations including electron-magnon interactions via the transverse T matrix.
why_interesting: The treatment of coupled, non-equilibrium dynamics involving multiple bosonic and fermionic excitations (magnons, phonons) within a Green's function framework is highly relevant to open quantum systems and non-equilibrium statistical mechanics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Diagrammatic representation124 of the transverse particle-hole T matrix Bethe-Salpeter equation in the Bloch space.

FIG. 2: Pictorial representation124 of the GT self-energy approximation as an emergent boson in proximity to a pole. Here, the wiggly red line represents the time-local screened interaction W, the black arrow represents the dressed electronic propagator, the grey arrow represents the bare electronic propagator and the wiggly double green line represents the magnon.

FIG. 3: Diagrammatic representation124 of the electron (a), phonon (b) and magnon (c) Dyson equation. Panel (d): electron self-energy. The black arrow represents the dressed electron propagator, the grey arrow represents the bare electron propagator, the single wiggly red line represents the bare phonon propagator, the double wiggly red line represents the dressed phonon propagator, the orange vertex represents the screened electron-phonon coupling, the single wiggly green line represents the bare magnon propagator, the double wiggly green line represents the dressed magnon propagator, the grey vertex represents the electron-magnon interaction, the double light-blue dashed line represents...
FIG. 4: Equations of motion for coupled electrons (light-blue rectangle), magnons (yellow rectangle) and phonons (red rectangle).
